render_data.title: How does the wave change if the source moves away?

options.note: Higher frequency / shorter wavelength — this is blue-shift, not red-shift.
Stretched out / longest wavelength / lowest frequency — CORRECT: a source moving away red-shifts the light.
Same frequency as the stationary source (peak first) — no shift.
Same frequency, inverted phase (trough first) — no change in wavelength.

render_data.concept: A source moving away stretches the wavelength and lowers the frequency (Doppler red-shift); the correct graph shows the most spread-out wave.

image_prompt: SVG: scatter graph, x-axis Distance / Mpc (0, 50, 100), y-axis Velocity / km s⁻¹ (0, 5000, 10⁴). Dense grey fan-shaped cloud proportional through the origin (gradient ~90 km/s/Mpc), plus a small magenta cluster of 'Galaxies observed in 1930' near the origin. Key/legend on the right: red = observed in 1930, grey = observed today. Shows direct proportionality (Hubble's law); later parts read off the gradient/age.

topic: Resistance vs length of a wire
image_prompt: SVG titled 'A graph of resistance against length for a conducting wire'. x-axis Length / m (0.00 to 1.20 step 0.20), y-axis Resistance / Ω (0.00 to 0.06 step 0.01). Five data points at (0.20,0.015), (0.40,0.027), (0.60,0.033), (0.80,0.045), (1.00,0.056), with a best-fit straight line of gradient ~0.045 Ω/m and a NON-ZERO y-intercept of ~0.0075 Ω (evidence of systematic error). Later parts compute the gradient and discuss the intercept.

render_data.title: Energy transformations as the child slides down
render_data.conservation: Total input 750 J = kinetic 672 J + sound 10 J + the missing form. The unlabelled band is gravitational potential energy = 80 J.

topic: Component X lab notebook (raw I-V readings)

render_data.title: Component X — measure the current at each voltage
render_data.task: Set the supply to 2.0 V and read the ammeter (601 mA = 0.60 A) to complete the notebook. Sweep the slider to see the ammeter and voltmeter update and R = V / I for each trial.

image_prompt: SVG graph titled 'A graph of current against voltage for component Y'. x-axis Voltage / V (0.0 to 10.0, major every 2.0, minor 0.5); y-axis Current / A (0.00 to 0.50, major every 0.05, minor 0.025). Smooth concave-down curve (non-ohmic) through (0,0),(1.0,0.20),(2.0,0.28),(3.0,0.33),(4.0,0.36),(5.0,0.38),(6.0,0.40),(7.0,0.42),(8.0,0.44) with cross markers. Read-offs used later: at V=1.0 ⇒ I=0.20 (R=5.0 Ω); at I=0.40 ⇒ V≈6.0 (R=15 Ω).

topic: Decorative lights scene + started series circuit

render_data.title: Build a series circuit to measure the current through two bulbs
render_data.prompt: The battery is already placed. Arrange the remaining components around the loop so two bulbs and the ammeter are all in series.

image_prompt: SVG infographic, three colour-coded panels each with an original illustration plus Description and Advantages text. (1) Wood burning biomass — trees + power station with steam; quick-growing trees farmed and burned for heat in a conventional station, woodland space; carbon neutral, cheap, easy to harness. (2) Geothermal power — pipework + borehole into hot rocks; water pumped down, heated by hot rocks, returned as steam to turn a turbine, earthquakes possible; carbon neutral, stable continuous supply, long-term planning, no polluting gases. (3) Tidal power — barrage with turbines in the sea + waves; tides captured through turbines, high up-front cost from tax-payer investment; carbon neutral, predictable, long-lasting, low maintenance.

SVG infographic on a dark sea panel: stylised Malta islands with a nuclear plant icon, three offshore wind turbines, and a dashed high-voltage cable running to a 'North Africa (solar farm)' point. Three data tables with EXACT values. Solar cable: Power 250 MW, Area 1 km², Cost/kWh €0.05, Construction €3.9 billion, Lifetime 25 yr, Decommission €1 million. Offshore wind: 250 MW, 73 km², €0.15, €1.6 billion, 15 yr, €30 million. Nuclear: 250 MW, 0.1 km², €0.02, €2.2 billion, 40 yr, €1000 million.
Source crop is a blank answer box / page chrome with no diagram — flagged as text_only; no SVG generated. Re-crop needed if a Brownian-motion figure is required.
Source crop is the SI-units answer table (text only, e.g. 'metre per second'), not a diagram — flagged as text_only; no SVG generated.
Source crop is the electron/positron mass table and surrounding question text, not a diagram — flagged as text_only; no SVG generated.
